The great war of the Bharatas or descendants of Bharat; the great narrative of the war of the Bharatas (name of the great epic poem — ascribed to Vyasa — describing the acts, rivalries, and contests of the Kurus and Pāṇḍus, two great collateral branches of the house of Bhārat, so called from Bharat its founder)
s.m. 'The great commentary'; name of Patanjali's great commentary on the grammatical sūtras of Pāṇini
adj. (f. -ā), Highly fortunate or blessed, very prosperous or happy; very auspicious; — exceedingly eminent, exalted, illustrious, highly distinguished; virtuous in a high degree, pure, holy
s.f., or mahā-bhāgatva, s.m. High excellence, exalted station or merit; great good fortune, or happiness, or prosperity; possession of the eight cardinal virtues
adj. (f. -inī) = mahā-bhāg, q.v.; — s.m. An exceedingly fortunate person, &c.
adj. (f. -ā) = mahā-bhāg, q.v.; — s.m. = mahā-bhāgatā, q.v.
s.m. Great danger or peril; great fear or dread
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Greatly terrified; very timid, pusillanimous, cowardly; — a very timid man; a great coward; — mahā-bhītā, s.f. A very timid woman; — a sort of sensitive plant, Mimosa pudica
s.m. A great crime, or sin; guilt of the most heinous kind (i.q. mahā-pātak, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Guilty of a great sin, or an atrocious crime; very criminal or guilty; — one guilty of heinous sin; a great criminal (i.q. mahā-pātakī, q.v.)

adj. Falling with great force; — s.m. A great crime or sin, a heinous offence, a crime of the highest degree (five such are enumerated in Manu's Code, viz. killing a Brāhmaṇ; drinking intoxicating liquors; theft; committing adultery with the wife of a spiritual teacher; and associating with anyone guilty of these crimes)
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Guilty of one of the five great crimes; — one guilty of heinous sin, a great criminal or offender, an atrocious sinner
s.m. A Buddhist lay-brother; a religious mendicant
vulg. mahā-pākhaṇḍ, s.m. Great heresy, or hypocrisy; — a great heretic; a great hypocrite; a great blasphemer

s.m. Principal path or road (to a place, or house), main entrance; chief road, or high street (in a town); main road, high road, highway; — the long journey, the passage into the next world, the way of all flesh
s.m. Name of one of the nine treasures of Kuvera; — name of one of Kuver's attendants; — name of one of the Nāgas; — a particular high number ( = one hundred thousand billions)
s.m. (in Gram.) An aspirated letter; aspirated pronunciation; spiritus asper
s.m. A great master, mighty lord, a great personage, king, prince, chief; a very holy man, or great saint; — an epithet of Indra, of Śiva, and of Vishṇu
s.m. 'A great present' of food, &c. (distributed among the persons present at the worship of an idol, especially of Jagannāth)

s.m. (f. -ī), A great man, an eminent personage, a magnate, chief; one invested with official authority; a dignitary; a great saint or sage, a reverend or holy person; a great ascetic; — (ironic.) a consummate knave

vulg. mahā-parlai, or mahā-pirlai, s.m. The total annihilation of the universe at the end of a Kalp; an entire dissolution and destruction of things after a period commensurate with the life of Brahma (when the seven Lokas and their inhabitants, together with all the saints, gods, and Brahmā himself, are annihilated); — the great deluge
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Very learned; — a great Panḍit, or philosopher, a great scholar; a great teacher
s.m. Deep mire; a great slough or quagmire
s.m. 'The great principle'; the intellect (or second of the Sānkhya Tattvas)
adj. (f. -ā), Very bitter, or sharp, or pungent; — s.m. The large nimb-tree, Melia sempervirens
s.m. Name of the sixth of the seven lower worlds or regions under the earth (inhabited by the Nāgas, Asurs, Daityas, and other evil beings)

or mahātmā (˚hā+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Of great soul, high-souled, lofty-minded, magnanimous, noble-minded, noble, high-bred, high-principled; generous, liberal; — high-souled or noble being; venerable or holy being, &c.
adj. Of great energy, or vigour; very vigorous, or very energetic; — of great splendour; full of fire; very bright
s.m. A great tīrth or place of pilgrimage
adj. (f. -ā), Exceedingly sharp (as a weapon, or perception, &c.); very pungent (as a flavour); — s.m. A very acute person
or (dialec.) mahā-jālā, s.m. A large fishing-net, a seine
adj. & s.m. Having a great jaṭā, q.v.; — an epithet of Śiva
s.f. A great braid or coil of hair; the matted or twisted hair of Śiva
or mahā-jaṛ, adj. Utterly irrational; intensely stupid, or insensible
s.m. (f. -ī), A great or eminent man, a very distinguished person; a good or trustworthy man; a man of credit; a banker, money-dealer; a merchant, a wholesale dealer
vulg. mahā-gyān, adj. (f. -ā), Possessed of great knowledge or learning; — very wise
s.f. Great risk, or peril; a great enterprise; a great achievement
s.m. (f. -dātri), A great giver, a very beneficent person
s.m. A great gift; an epithet of certain valuable gifts, or the giving of different kinds of valuable presents to the priests (sixteen such gifts are particularly enumerated)
adj. (f. -ā), Exceedingly vile, exceptionally bad; — s.m. An exceedingly vile person, a great miscreant
s.m. A large tooth, or tusk; an elephant's tusk; — adj. & s.m. Having large teeth, or tusks; — an elephant with large tusks
s.m. 'The great metal, or element'; gold
vulg. mahā-deʼo, s.m. 'The great deity,' an epithet of Śiva (the third deity of the Hindū triad; it is the name by which he is most commonly known)
s.f. 'The great goddess,' especially as an epithet of Durgā, or Pārvatī (the wife of Śiva)
s.m. A large and splendid sort of sedan (or pālkī) for great personages
s.f. = mahā-rātri; and corr. of mahā-rāshṭrī, qq.v.
s.m., or mahā-rātri, s.f. Midnight, the dead of night; late at night; time after midnight; close of night
s.f. The longest night in the year; the great night of the complete destruction of the world; — the eighth day (or night) in the light half of the month Aśvin

s.m. A great king; reigning prince, supreme sovereign, paramount power (i.q. mahā-rājā); — a form of address to a Brāhmaṇ, or a superior; your Majesty! your Excellency! your Honour! Sir! — mahā-rājā, s.m. Supreme power, sovereign, emperor
(˚ja+adh˚), s.m. Lord paramount, king of kings, universal emperor
s.m. The rank or title of a reigning sovereign; sovereignty, dominion
s.m. A great kingdom or realm; 'the great country,' i.e. the Marhaṭṭā country; — name of a kind of metre in Sanskrit prosody
s.m. pl. The Mahraṭṭā people, the Mahraṭṭās
or mahā-rāshṭī, s.f. The Mahraṭṭī language; — s.m. A Marhaṭṭā Brāhmaṇ
s.m. The great Rāmāyan, one of the many Rāmāyaṇs of Vālmīki
or mahā-rānī, s.f. A great queen; the principal wife of a Rājā; a queen in her own right, reigning queen; empress; — a respectful form of address to Hindū ladies of rank
s.m. (f. -nī, or inī), A great traveller
s.m. A large car, or vehicle; a great chariot; — one who has a great chariot, a great warrior or hero

vulg. mahā-sāntpan, s.m. lit. 'Great tormenting'; a kind of severe penance (viz. subsisting for six successive days respectively on cow's urine, cow-dung, milk, curds, ghī, and water in which Kuśa-grass has been boiled, and fasting on the seventh day; or, instead of one day, some authorities assign a period of three days to each penance, considering the first kind as the common sāntpan; others omit the sixth and seventh penance, making the whole last fifteen days)
s.m. Excessive violence, great cruelty or outrage, brutal assault; — extreme daring or audacity
s.f. A great, or royal, council; a senate
adj. & s.m. Having a great or noble essence; noble, good, virtuous, just; — a noble being; a virtuous person
s.m. Great pleasure, excessive enjoyment; — copulation, coition
s.f. A very lovely woman, a great beauty
s.m. The skull; the temporal or frontal bone, the forehead; — the highest or last number of numeration (i.q. mahā-śankh)
adj. (f. -ā), Very fine, or minute; very subtile; very delicate
(˚hā+ash˚), s.f. 'The great eighth,' the eighth day of the light half of the month Asin (or festival in honour of Durgā, called the Durgā-pūjā)
s.m. A particular high number, one thousand billions, or a thousand millions; — one of Kuver's treasures; — the temporal or frontal bone, the forehead

(˚hā+āś˚), adj. & s.m. Having a noble disposition, high-minded, magnanimous, liberal, munificent; open, unsuspicious; — a noble-minded man; a respectable person, a gentleman; — a term of respectful address; your Honour! Sir! Master! — mahā-kāl, s.m. A form of Śiva; an epithet of Śiva in his character of the destroying deity (he being then represented of a black colour, and of more or less terrific aspect
s.f. A form, or an epithet, of Durgā (wife of Śiva), in her terrific form
adj. (f. -ā) 'Large-bodied,' of great stature, tall, large, gigantic; bulky, stout
s.m. A great work; — adj. Accomplishing great works; doing mighty deeds; — an epithet of Śiva
or mahā-kūl, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of a great family, sprung from a noble family, high-born, noble, aristocratic; — a man of noble or good family, a noble
s.m. Name of a very large esculent root; — a sort of yam; — garlic
s.m. A large or deep well
s.m. 'Great cutaneous eruption'; the worst form of leprosy
s.m. A great bay, or creek; — inroad of the sea upon the adjoining low ground
s.m. 'The great division (of the earth),' an epithet of Bhārat-varsh
s.m. 'Great antidote,' a kind of medicinal compound; — great sickness, severe illness; fever; — adj. Armed with a great club
(˚hā+āl˚), s.m. A great dwelling; a great temple, or monastery; a place of refuge, sanctuary, asylum; — the great refuge, the Supreme Being, or great Universal Spirit
s.m. 'Great iron'; a magnet, a loadstone

or mahā-māri, or mahā-mārī, s.f. Great pestilence, or mortality; great slaughter or havoc; plague; epidemic disease; cholera-morbus; — mahā-mārī, s.f. 'The great destroying goddess,' an epithet of Durgā
s.m. A great pestilence; a great battle; great slaughter (i.q. mahā-māri); — a great fighter; a hard hitter
s.m. A kind of large bean, Dolichos catsang
s.m. 'Costly meat'; human flesh (one who takes money on giving his daughter in marriage is said to sell mahā-māṅs or 'human flesh')
s.f. One of the seven sisters who are goddesses of small-pox, &c.

s.f. Great deceit, or illusion; worldly illusion; the divine power of illusion (which makes the material universe appear as if really existing, and renders it cognizable by the senses); the Great Illusion (the illusory nature of worldly objects personified); an epithet of the goddess Durgā
s.m. & f. Costly gem; precious jewel (as the diamond, ruby, &c.)
s.m., or mahā-murkhatā, s.f. Exceeding folly
adj. (f. -ā), High-priced; very costly, or precious, or valuable; — s.m. A ruby
s.m. Great confusion or infatuation of mind; great illusion; great ignorance
or mahā-mahimā, adj. & s.m. Very, or extremely, great; truly great; high and mighty; — excessive greatness, omnipotence; true greatness
s.m. The coral-tree, Erythrina indica; — a species of medicinal plant; a particular drug (classed among the principal drugs, and described as a tonic and stimulant)
(˚hā+anu˚), adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. In high esteem, worthy, mighty, exalted, dignified, pre-eminent, just, virtuous; — a gentleman
s.m. Name of one of the twenty-one hells or places of torment
s.f. Midnight; the dead of night, the time after midnight
s.m. A great city; — name of a particular city
s.m. A species of large nimb-tree, or Melia sempervirens
(˚hā+ān˚), s.m. Great bliss, the great joy of deliverance from further transmigration; final emancipation, or beatitude; — a proper name
(fr. mahānand, q.v.), adj. Relating to Mahānand; — relating to final emancipation; — s.m. One who has attained final beatitude
vulg. mahā-naʼumī, or mahā-naumī, s.f. The ninth day in the light half of the month Asin; the festival held on that day
s.m. (lit. 'dark, or deep, blue') A kind of sapphire; — name of one of the Nāgas

s.m. Any long continuous composition or literary work, a magnum opus; — principal sentence, great proposition; epithet of twelve mystical utterances of the Upanishads (esp. of the mystic words Tattvam, and Om)
s.m. Deep thought, or consideration; profound meditation
s.m. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus
adj. & s.m. Very poisonous or venomous; — the Coluber Nāga, or a kind of small and very venomous serpent (said to have two heads)
(for mahā-vishuv-sankrānt), s.f. The vernal equinox, the moment of the sun's passing into Aries (differing by several days from European computation)
s.m. A great wood; a large, or dense, forest
s.m. Name of one of the twenty-one hells or places of torment

s.m. A great hero; — a lion; — an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Garuḍ (the bird and vehicle of Vishṇu); — fire, sacrificial fire; a sacrificial vessel; — the thunder-bolt of Indra; — a white horse; — name of the last or twenty-fourth Arhat (the last and most celebrated Jain teacher of the present age; supposed to have flourished in the province of Behār in the sixth century before the Christian era)
adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Very energetic; very powerful, or mighty; — a name of Brahmā; — a Brāhmaṇ
s.m. Great laughter; loud laughter; a horse-laugh

vulg. mahā-yagya, s.m. A great sacrifice or offering; a principal act of devotion, a sacrament (of the Hindū religion: of these acts five are enumerated; and they are severally considered as due to, 1˚ the Vedas; 2˚ the gods; 3˚ man; 4˚ the manes; 5˚ all created beings. The acts themselves are, 1˚ the study of Scripture; 2˚ the offering sacrifice to the gods; 3˚ hospitality to guests; 4˚ libations of water to the manes of deceased ancestors; 5˚ the casting of food on the ground, or into water, as an offering to created beings generally)
adj. Very glorious, very famous or illustrious, renowned, celebrated
vulg. mahā-jug, s.m. A great yug, a yug of the gods, the aggregate of the four yugs, Krita, Tretā, Dvāpar, and Kali ( = 4,320,000,000 years of mortals)
vulg. mahā-jantr, s.m. A great machine; a great mechanical work (as a lock, a dike, &c.).

Love for an object, interested affection; cupboard love
